top_signals.title: EU Drops Sanctions on Lukoil Founder and Russian Patriarch
top_signals.summary: The European Union will not impose sanctions on the head of the Russian Orthodox Church and the chief of Russia’s largest private oil company, after Bulgaria vetoed the proposal. Patriarch Kirill, a vocal supporter of President Vladimir Putin and public backer of the war on Ukraine, had been expected to be included in the latest round of financial penalties that the 27-member bloc was considering. Kirill “consistently justified and supported Russia’s aggressive war against Ukraine,” calling it “sacred,” according…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Chip Selloff Rocks Tech Stocks | Open Interest 7/13/2026
top_signals.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Markets hit another tech speed bump as SK Hynix sparks a chip selloff, while Wall Street braces for bank earnings and critical inflation data. Oil jumps after fresh strikes and President Trump vows to take over the Strait of Hormuz. Plus, exclusive interviews with Sunrun CEO Mary Powell on turning homes into AI hubs, General Fusion CEO Greg Twinney after the company's Nasdaq debut, KBW's Chris McGratty on the biggest bank earnings risks, and Lazard's George Bilicic on powering the AI boom. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Oil prices jump nearly 8% after Trump reinstates Strait of Hormuz blockade on Iranian ships
top_signals.summary: The U.S. and Iran disputed whether Hormuz was open to ship traffic after trading strikes over the weekend.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Ryuk operator pleads guilty; Blackcat/AlphV conspirator gets nearly 6-year sentence
top_signals.summary: One man accused of deploying Ryuk ransomware pleaded guilty Wednesday in an Oregon federal court to conspiracy and computer fraud, while another man received a 70-month federal prison sentence in a Florida court for helping the Blackcat/AlphV gang extort multiple victims.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: UK tokenization push could add as much as $44B to annual output by 2035: Report
top_signals.summary: The government-backed roadmap calls for the UK’s first digital gilt by early 2027 and seeks to make tokenized bonds usable for trading and borrowing.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.
